term: catunnaṁ ariyasaccānaṁ ācikkhanā desanā paññāpanā paṭṭhapanā vivaraṇā vibhajanā uttānīkammaṁ
preferred_translation: the explaining, teaching, laying out, establishing, opening up, analyzing, and making clear of the four noble truths
alternative_translations: the explaining, teaching, setting out, establishing, opening up, analyzing, and making clear of the four noble truths
discouraged_translations: the doctrine of the four noble truths
the revelation of the four truths
definition: The analytical opening formula that frames MN 141 as an explicit breakdown of the four noble truths.
notes: Use this record when the multi-verb analytical opening in MN 141 needs to be translated as a governed unit. Keep the sequence active and editorially plain rather than flattening it into a single abstract noun like exposition.

example_phrases.pali: catunnaṁ ariyasaccānaṁ ācikkhanā desanā paññāpanā paṭṭhapanā vivaraṇā vibhajanā uttānīkammaṁ
example_phrases.translation: the explaining, teaching, laying out, establishing, opening up, analyzing, and making clear of the four noble truths
example_phrases.notes: The governing analytical opening in MN 141.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the multi-verb analytical opening that frames the discourse as an explicit breakdown of the four truths.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use this as the standard English control line for MN 141's analytical opening unless a recorded local reason requires minor grammatical adjustment.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not collapse the sequence into generic teaching-language, and do not make the line sound ceremonial or archaic.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the title formula of MN 141 from drifting into vague exposition-language or inflated revelation-language.
